      Community Developer EST: 2010
             PvP emphisized creations for your deathly pleasure to be reborn
      Do you like "      [Perdition's Reprieve]"? Click the star in the bottom left corner! Or keep me inspired with a donation @Paypal
      Dagradt, May 30, 2014 Report
      #10 Like Reply

    11. Dagradt Community Developer

      Joined:
      Jul 26, 2010
      Messages:
      1,423
      Likes Received:
      44
      Trophy Points:
      48
      Overview:
      -       In this section I will show you how to       setup your rotation using a priority system and add the Conditions.cs file methods we created.

      Requirements:
      - Current Honorbuddy release
      - Microsoft Visual Studios Express 2013 for Windows Desktop
      - Conditions.cs file
      - Hotkey Manager.cs file
      - Spell Book.cs file
      - Talent Mananger.cs file
      - Unit.cs file

      [HR][/HR]
      >> Lesson ten: Cast the Priority <<

      ​
      Alright, since a numerous amount of requests are coming in to start this section I will go ahead but, it will be done in sections and I don't want to hear a peep out of anyone! Grab your "Rotation.cs" file and add in the following three regions:
      PHP:
      using System;
      using System.Collections.Generic;
      using System.Linq;
      using System.Text;
      using System.Threading.Tasks;

      namespace       Death_Knight_Frost__1H.Rotation
      {
          class       Rotation
                {
                    #region [Method] - Combat Buffing
              #endregion

              #region [Method] - Precombat Buffing
              #endregion

              #region [Method] - Rotation Selector
              #endregion
                }
      }
      The following code for each of the above regions is basically the same with subtlety variations in name, validation checks, and of course spells. Starting with the top(Combat Buffing), add in the below code the the correct region:

      *Combat Buffing

      PHP:
      public static Composite combatBuffing()
      {
          return new       PrioritySelector(
              new       Decorator(ret => HkM.manualOn || !Me.IsAlive || !Me.GotTarget, new ActionAlwaysSucceed())
              );
      }
      *Precombat Buffing

      PHP:
      public static Composite preCombatBuffing()
      {
          return new       PrioritySelector(
              new       Decorator(ret => HkM.manualOn || !Me.IsAlive || Me.IsCasting || Me.IsChanneling || Me.Mounted || Me.OnTaxi, new ActionAlwaysSucceed())
              );
      }
      As you can see in the above code, we haven't changed much besides the name and a few different validation checks. We use the same two start checks to ensure that we are not set to manual and that we are alive. After that we check what is needed given the time in which we will be casting. For combat buffing we check to make sure we have a target and for precombat buffing we remove this for a check to make sure we aren't casting or flying around.

      *Rotation Selector

      PHP:
      public static Composite rotationSelector()
      {
          return new       PrioritySelector(
              new       Decorator(ret => HkM.manualOn || !Me.IsAlive || !Me.GotTarget, new ActionAlwaysSucceed()),
              new Switch<>(      ret => HkM.aoeOn,
                  new       SwitchArgument<>(true,
                      new       PrioritySelector(
                          )),
                  new       SwitchArgument<>(false,
                      new       PrioritySelector(
                          ))));
      }
      *Please note: Make sure you add the word "bool" Inside the "<>"! My HTLM is still broken and it erases the phrase when I type it out as it should be

      Now here I switch it up. I've used the same checks as the combat buffing section but, I've added in a swapping method to determine whether we should be doing the "AoE" rotation or the "Single Target" rotation. Upon "HKM.aoeOn" returning "true" or "false", the "Switch" function will run the desired code via the "SwitchArgument". There are various other ways to do this but, I feel this one is easier to read and the speed is the same when checked against simpler(?) methods.

      Now clear out those red doom lines and add in:

      PHP:
      private static LocalPlayer Me { get { return StyxWoW.Me; } }
      Now add in our "Class Redundancy" links and you should be set to add in your spells:

      PHP:
      #region [Method] - Class Redundancy
      using C = Death_Knight_Frost__1H.Rotation.Conditions;
      using HkM = Death_Knight_Frost__1H.Core.Managers.Hotkey_Manager;
      using SB = Death_Knight_Frost__1H.Core.Helpers.Spell_Book;
      using TM = Death_Knight_Frost__1H.Core.Managers.Talent_Manager;
      using U = Death_Knight_Frost__1H.Core.Unit;
      #endregion
      *You might be wondering why I've added in so many redundancies. This is because we will use all of these later on and I simply want to get it out of the way now

      At this point you should look like:

      PHP:
      using CommonBehaviors.Actions;
      using Styx;
      using Styx.TreeSharp;
      using Styx.WoWInternals.WoWObjects;
      using System;
      using System.Collections.Generic;
      using System.Linq;
      using System.Text;
      using System.Threading.Tasks;

      #region [Method] - Class Redundancy
      using C = Death_Knight_Frost__1H.Rotation.Conditions;
      using HkM = Death_Knight_Frost__1H.Core.Managers.Hotkey_Manager;
      using SB = Death_Knight_Frost__1H.Core.Helpers.Spell_Book;
      using TM = Death_Knight_Frost__1H.Core.Managers.Talent_Manager;
      using U = Death_Knight_Frost__1H.Core.Unit;
      #endregion

      namespace Death_Knight_Frost__1H.Rotation
      {
          class       Rotation
                {
              private static       LocalPlayer Me { get { return StyxWoW.Me; } }

                    #region [Method] - Combat Buffing
                    public static Composite combatBuffing()
              {
                  return new       PrioritySelector(
                      new       Decorator(ret => HkM.manualOn || !Me.IsAlive || !Me.GotTarget, new ActionAlwaysSucceed())
                      );
              }
                    #endregion

              #region [Method] - Precombat Buffing
                    public static Composite preCombatBuffing()
              {
                  return new       PrioritySelector(
                      new       Decorator(ret => HkM.manualOn || !Me.IsAlive || Me.IsCasting || Me.IsChanneling || Me.Mounted || Me.OnTaxi, new ActionAlwaysSucceed())
                      );
              }
                    #endregion

              #region [Method] - Rotation Selector
                    public static Composite rotationSelector()
              {
                  return new       PrioritySelector(
                      new       Decorator(ret => HkM.manualOn || !Me.IsAlive || !Me.GotTarget, new ActionAlwaysSucceed()),
                      new Switch<>(      ret => HkM.aoeOn,
                          new       SwitchArgument<>(true,
                              new       PrioritySelector(
                                  )),
                          new       SwitchArgument<>(false,
                              new       PrioritySelector(
                                  ))));
              }
                    #endregion
                }
      }
      *Remember to add "bool" inside the "<>"

      Okay, for ease of conveying we'll use the same method as we did in lesson five for the remaining section of this lesson. I will be using the Death Knight one hand rotation from "SimulationCraft" as before to determine the priority I should be using throughout the Combat Routine. You can find the link to the site below in the references section, again please note that these are tier 16 heroic rotations.

      Now what we are going to do is copy and past all of the rotations into the respective areas and comment them out so that we don't get a billion errors. Remember the usage of "//" for commenting out a section or if doing a bulk omission you can simply highlight the text and press "Ctrl+E" let go of this key combination and then press "C".

      *Please note: for undoing a bulk omission the same process can be used but, use "U" in lue of "C"

      Go ahead and copy in your rotation and don't forget to comment it out. Now you need to filter all the mess you have down to just the spells needed within the rotation(you don't need "actions.precombat+=/snapshot_stats", remove it), I trust you can do this part on your own but, here's an example just in case you are still a pathetic fail of a n00b:

      actions.precombat
      - Goes under the new Decorator which is under the Precombat-PrioitySelector
      actions
      - Goes under the new Decorator which is under the Combat-PrioritySelector
      actions.aoe
      - Goes under the AoE-PrioritySelector
      actions.single
      - Goes under the Single Target-PrioritySelector

      Once you've filtered all the unneeded parts, ensure you have everything in the correct area. You should look something similar to this and if not you should probably just stop now:




      *Here we will stop until we have lesson nine finished. Consider this the end of lesson ten part one.

      Okay, taking what we have done in lesson nine and the above setup. I am going to show you a quick means to continue our implementation from lesson nine:

      *Blood Tap
      PHP:
      //blood_tap,if=talent.blood_tap.enabled
      new Decorator(ret => TM.bloodTap, C.bloodTap())
      *Death and Decay
      PHP:
      //death_and_decay,if=unholy=1
      new Decorator(ret => U.unholyRunes == 1, C.deathandDecay(currentTarget, 30))
      *Horn of Winter
      PHP:
      //horn_of_winter
      new Decorator(ret => true, C.hornofWinter()),
      *Obliterate
      PHP:
      //obliterate,if=unholy>0&!buff.killing_machine.react
      new Decorator(ret => U.unholyRunes > 0 && !U.auraExists(Me, SB.auraKillingMachine), C.Obliterate(currentTarget, 5)),
      Now remember that I also told you that you could skip all of the above for an easier method? Below you will find the alternative method you can use to achieve the same thing but, you will have to add any extra checks to the function here instead of in the "Conditions.cs" file which you will no longer need if the below method is chosen:

      *Blood Tap
      PHP:
      //blood_tap,if=talent.blood_tap.enabled
      S.blindCast(SB.spellBloodTap, ret => U.bloodCharge > 4 && (U.bloodRunes == 0 || U.frostRunes == 0 || U.unholyRunes == 0) && TM.bloodTap, true);
      *Death and Decay
      PHP:
      //death_and_decay,if=unholy=1
      S.dropCast(SB.spellDeathandDecay, ret => currentTarget, ret => U.isUnitValid(currentTarget, 30) && U.unholyRunes == 1);
      *Horn of Winter
      PHP:
      //horn_of_winter
      S.Buff(SB.spellHornofWinter, ret => true)
      *Obliterate
      PHP:
      //obliterate,if=unholy>0&!buff.killing_machine.react
      S.Cast(SB.spellObliterate, ret => U.isUnitValid(currentTarget, 5) && U.unholyRunes > 0 && !U.auraExists(Me, SB.auraKillingMachine)),
      See the difference? The choice is yours and the speed isn't noticeable, so go forth and start your creation!

      [HR][/HR]
      Materials:
      - Honorbuddy
      -       Microsoft Visual Studios Express 2013 for Windows Desktop

      Resources:
      - SimulationCraft
